events.summary: Site reported multiple bots not moving after a heartbeat issue.

events.summary: Additional site impact reported: totes could not be removed from hospital and question marks were visible on the HMI.

events.summary: Site contact reported that nothing was moving during normal operation and there was no fault or failed AGV visible.
events.details: Jinhao Liu stated on 2/20 that he was talking with Garrett from site, who said the issue was that nothing was moving. The message also says there was no fault or any failed AGVs and that this happened during normal operation.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: hi guys, i am talking with garrett ... he said the issue is that nothing ... there was no fault or any failed AGVs. this happened during normal operation also

uncertainties: Part of the sentence is truncated by OCR.
Garrett's full identity/role is not fully established in this chunk.

events.summary: Memory trend was shared for review.
events.details: Page 7 shows Zane Bubb posting a memory trend in chat. Supporting artifact evidence from page 8 shows a memory chart with initially high usage followed by a sharp drop and sustained lower plateau.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Zane Bubb 2/20 9:17 AM Memory Trend
Memory Trend chart shows initially high memory usage followed by a sharp drop to a lower sustained level.

uncertainties: The chart does not itself confirm cause of crash.

events.summary: Site was finishing bagout and troubleshooting would resume after the e-stop was removed.
events.details: Christopher White said the site was currently finishing the bagout process and they needed to wait for them to finish and un-e-stop the system before rejoining the troubleshooting call and continuing.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: site is currently finishing the bagout process ... once the estop is removed, we will all rejoin the troubleshooting call

events.summary: Memory review found no indication that memory caused a crash.
events.details: Michael Langley responded to the shared memory trend that there was no indication memory caused a crash and suggested checking the CPU trend to see if anything there looked problematic.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: No indication here that memory caused a crash. You can also check the CPU trend
Memory Trend chart shows a sharp drop and lower sustained level.

uncertainties: The visible timestamp on Michael Langley's message may be OCR-distorted.
This is an attributed diagnostic conclusion, not definitive root cause.

events.summary: Restart guidance was shared for use if Ignition needed to be restarted.
events.details: Page text provides conditional instructions: first E-stop the system, restart Ignition, wait for it to come back up, then verify the OptiSweep service is responding with a Get Agv Statuses request.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: In the event that you do need to restart it here are the steps... First Estop the system... restart ignition... verify the OptiSweep service is still responding to request after Ignition is restarted

uncertainties: This is guidance shared in the source, not proof that all steps were executed at this point.

events.summary: An Ignition gateway restart command was executed.
events.details: Command prompt shows navigation to the Ignition installation directory, an unsuccessful `ls -1` attempt, then execution of `gwcmd -r`. Final visible output says `Waiting for Gateway restart...`.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: C:\Program Files\Inductive Automation\Ignition>gwcmd -r Naiting for Gateway restart...

uncertainties: The screenshot does not show the final restart outcome.

events.summary: Ignition Systems Overview page was captured showing the gateway responding.
events.details: Status page at localhost:8088/web/status/sys.performance shows CPU and memory widgets, current response time of 11 ms, and `Recent Slow Response Events` with `No events to display`. A `License Incomplete` banner is also visible.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Status > Systems > Overview ... License Incomplete ... Current Response Time ... No events to display

uncertainties: No visible timestamp is shown for when this status page was captured.
The incident relevance of the `License Incomplete` banner is unclear.

events.summary: GetAgvStatuses API check returned data successfully.
events.details: API client screenshot shows a GET request to `/GetAgvStatuses` with HTTP 200 and returned JSON records. Supporting artifact notes a validation warning that the response differed from the endpoint specification.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Get Agv Statuses ... http://10.27.80.165:5000/GetAgvStatuses ... NORMAL

uncertainties: The screenshot supports successful API response, but the schema mismatch warning may or may not be incident-relevant.

events.summary: A webservice application fault was attributed in the source to memory usage and log growth concerns.

Visible event text includes Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Database.Command and 'Failed executing DbCommand (1,840ms)'. Multiple red error entries are visible at the same timestamp.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Source: WCSWebApplication Logged: 2/20/2026 6:35:24 AM Event ID: 20102 Level: Error ... Failed executing DbCommand (1,840ms)
Event Properties dialog for WCSWebApplication Event ID 20102 shows Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Database.Command and failed DbCommand execution.

uncertainties: OCR is noisy for some parameter details in the DbCommand text.
The screenshot shows application errors but not full operational impact.

events.summary: System log records OptiSweep service terminating unexpectedly with automatic restart scheduled.
events.details: Event Viewer System log shows Event 7031 from Service Control Manager at 2/20/2026 6:36:02 AM. The visible message states that the OptiSweep service terminated unexpectedly and that corrective action will be taken in 60000 milliseconds to restart the service.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Event Properties - Event 7031, Service Control Manager ... The OptiSweep service terminated unexpectedly ... corrective action will be taken in 60000 milliseconds: Restart the ...
Event 7031 from Service Control Manager states the OptiSweep service terminated unexpectedly and will be restarted in 60000 milliseconds.

uncertainties: The screenshot documents the failure and configured restart action, but not whether restart later succeeded.
